steps.rationale: Keep the stronger generator model but move from SOAP to a more detailed note style so the note is less templated and more likely to retain full negatives, vitals, allergy context, and explicit precautions.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact captures the general clinical picture of a 3-day viral upper respiratory illness, but it falls well short of the expected note in fidelity and chart-ready quality. The biggest problems are omitted clinical details, a weakened and nonspecific plan without explicit return precautions, and invented or meta-evaluative language that does not belong in a clinical note.

steps.rationale: Swap to the alternate ASR path and ease preprocessing slightly to reduce transcript-artifact leakage while preserving low-volume clinical details such as meds, allergies, and return precautions.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves the broad clinical picture but misses too many important facts and does not deliver a sufficiently specific, chart-ready follow-up note. Its main weaknesses are factual omissions, missing return precautions, and unprofessional transcript leakage, which collectively make it materially weaker than the expected note despite having a recognizable SOAP structure.

steps.rationale: Test whether tighter speech segmentation with the same high-capability note model improves factual extraction and removes stray foreign-language fragments without changing the overall SOAP framing.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact preserves the broad clinical picture of a likely viral upper respiratory infection, but it misses too many clinically important details and underdelivers on plan specificity and return precautions. Its chart readiness is further reduced by transcript leakage, foreign-language fragments, and self-referential commentary about note quality, so overall it falls well short of the expected note despite capturing the main diagnosis.
